meta_title: For Founders: portfolio building to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Portfolio Building to Solve Project Stalling
content.seo_description: Stop starting and start shipping. Use this founder-focused checklist to build a high-impact portfolio using Antigravity and Claude Code to finish projects.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing the perfect idea and start building a high-velocity portfolio. By decomposing complex SaaS features into micro-modules using Antigravity and Claude Code, you shift from 'infinite project mode' to a 'shipping mindset.' This strategy forces rapid validation, ensures technical completion, and builds the tangible proof required to attract paying customers.
content.intro:
The graveyard of unfinished SaaS projects is paved with good intentions and feature creep. You start with high-octane energy, but as the architecture grows, the momentum fades into a paralyzing fog of debugging and scope expansion. You aren't lacking talent; you are lacking a system that forces completion.

Portfolio building is your antidote. By treating every project as a discrete, high-value asset rather than a lifelong commitment, you transform your workflow from aimless exploration into a structured, revenue-generating engine. We are moving away from 'learning' and toward 'shipment.'

steps.title: Define the Micro-SaaS MVP
steps.description: Identify a single, high-value problem that can be solved in 7 days. Focus on one core feature that provides immediate utility.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Help me define a 7-day MVP scope for a [Insert Idea] that includes one core feature, one database table, and one external API integration. Keep it strictly lean.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Outline the database schema and core logic for a 7-day MVP of [Insert Idea].'

steps.title: Setup the Scaffolding
steps.description: Initialize a robust project structure that enforces discipline and modularity using automated tooling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready Next.js project structure with Tailwind, Supabase integration, and a modular folder architecture for scalability.

steps.title: Implement Core Logic via AI Agents
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write your business logic in small, testable chunks to prevent architectural bloat.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the server-side logic for [Insert Feature] using TypeScript. Ensure error handling, input validation, and clean interface patterns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Implement the authentication middleware and core business logic for the main dashboard feature.'

steps.title: Rapid UI Integration
steps.description: Connect your logic to a functional UI, prioritizing user flow over aesthetic perfection to reach the 'Demo Day' stage faster.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive React component for [Insert Feature]. Connect it to the backend I just built and handle loading/error states.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Add a form component that interacts with the backend and displays real-time validation feedback.'

steps.title: Deployment and Feedback Loop
steps.description: Deploy to a live environment immediately. Your project isn't 'finished' until it is accessible by a user.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a deployment checklist for Vercel/Supabase, including environment variables, build commands, and basic smoke tests for my MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'npm run build && npx vercel --prod'

pro_tips: Treat your code as a disposable asset: if it takes more than 24 hours to debug, delete it and rewrite the module from scratch.
Force a 'Friday Ship' deadline: no matter the state, push the code to a public URL every Friday at 5 PM.
Document your failures in a README: technical debt is only a problem if it's hidden; expose it to learn faster.
Build for the 'One-User' test: if one person can use your tool to solve a problem, you have a product, not a project.
Use Claude Code to run unit tests automatically after every major file change to ensure your MVP doesn't break during iteration.
content.next_steps: Once your first MVP is live, gather feedback from 5 potential users, document the limitations, and decide whether to pivot or iterate before starting your next portfolio piece.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the framework to stop stalling and start shipping. Every day you don't ship is a day your competitors gain ground. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
